external_resources.label: Gene Ontology
notes: GENtle treats GO as an external resource namespace for mappings and provenance, not as the only authority for lab-facing gene groups.
A GENtle group may map to GO terms where useful, but local groups may also exist without GO coverage.

groups.label: Yamanaka factors
groups.long_definition: Canonical core reprogramming / stemness-associated transcription factors used as a lab shorthand for induced pluripotency workflows: OCT4/POU5F1, SOX2, KLF4, and MYC. GENtle stores this as a local gene group because it is experimentally useful even though it is not itself a public ontology term.

members.evidence_note: Canonical OCT4/POU5F1 member of the OSKM reprogramming-factor shorthand.
members.provenance: GENtle built-in local-knowledge seed.

members.evidence_note: Canonical SOX2 member of the OSKM reprogramming-factor shorthand.
members.provenance: GENtle built-in local-knowledge seed.

members.provenance: GENtle built-in local-knowledge seed.

members.evidence_note: Canonical MYC member of the OSKM reprogramming-factor shorthand.
members.provenance: GENtle built-in local-knowledge seed.

groups.provenance: Seeded from GENtle's previous built-in TF-query group so existing Yamanaka/stemness motif behavior can move toward catalog-backed resolution.
notes: This is a GENtle-owned lab-facing group, not a GO term.
GO annotations may describe individual member functions, but this shorthand remains local catalog knowledge.

groups.label: p53 family
groups.short_description: Canonical p53 transcription-factor family represented in the local motif registry.
groups.long_definition: Human p53 transcription-factor family shorthand covering TP53, TP63, and TP73 for motif, promoter, and local family-comparison workflows.

members.evidence_note: Canonical p53-family member.
members.provenance: GENtle built-in local-knowledge seed.

members.evidence_note: Canonical p53-family member.

members.evidence_note: Canonical p53-family member.

groups.provenance: Seeded from GENtle's previous built-in TF-query group.
notes: This family shorthand can be mapped to external resources later, but the local group is independently useful for promoter and motif inspection.

groups.label: Regulation of alternative splicing
groups.short_description: Curated starter group for RNA-binding and splicing-regulatory genes commonly used when discussing regulation of alternative splicing.
groups.long_definition: Starter GENtle gene group for lab-facing discussion of genes whose products are commonly treated as regulators of alternative exon inclusion, splice-site selection, or cell-state-specific alternative mRNA splicing. This group is anchored to GO:0000381 but is not a full GO annotation import; it is a compact, reviewable local seed intended for promoter/regulatory, RNA-read, and cohort questions.

members.evidence_note: RNA-binding FOX-family splicing regulator often used as a canonical alternative-splicing regulator.
members.provenance: GENtle built-in local-knowledge seed anchored to GO:0000381.

external_mappings.label: regulation of alternative mRNA splicing, via spliceosome
external_mappings.note: GENtle uses this as an external ontology anchor, not as a complete imported GO annotation set.

groups.provenance: AI-assisted GENtle seed, anchored to GO:0000381 and intended for review/extension through catalog overlays.
notes: This is intentionally compact and not a replacement for a full GO annotation import.
Use project/user overlays to add cell-type-specific or experiment-specific splicing regulators.
